ai_research.headline: Tesla Robotaxi goes fully driverless in Dallas + Houston — Agentic AI floods enterprise but Box CEO says 'obsolete in months'

items.title: Tesla unsupervised Robotaxi expands to Dallas and Houston
items.body: Tesla's fully driverless Robotaxi service (no human supervisors) launched in Dallas and Houston, joining Austin. Uses Model Y Juniper SUVs with FSD AI vision. Pricing: 56% cheaper than Waymo ($6.15 vs $13.93 for 2.25 miles). Three Texas cities now operational ahead of Q1 earnings April 22.

items.title: Box CEO: 'Systems you built aren't useful anymore' — agents obsolete in months
items.body: Aaron Levie warns that AI agents face rapid obsolescence due to evolving models. Teams must rebuild constantly as underlying capabilities shift. Counterpoint to the 'agents are the future' narrative — the half-life of agent architectures is shrinking faster than the development cycle.

items.title: Recursive Superintelligence raises $500M at $4B valuation (4 months old)
items.body: Backed by GV and NVIDIA, the 4-month-old lab focuses on advanced agentic systems. Signals investor appetite for next-gen agent infrastructure despite obsolescence warnings. One of the fastest seed-to-unicorn trajectories in AI.

ai_research.learn_today: The tension between 'agents are eating the world' and 'agents are obsolete in months' is the real story. Both are true simultaneously — the underlying models improve so fast that agent architectures built today may not make sense in 6 months. The winning play is building on abstractions that survive model upgrades, not optimizing for today's capabilities.

openclaw_agents.headline: Agent deployment accelerating across industries — Chinese workers training AI doubles, Coinbase going Slack-native

items.title: Chinese workers training 'AI doubles' to handle routine tasks
items.body: Employees are training AI agents that mimic them for emails, news summarization, and routine work. The agent becomes a digital proxy, not just a tool. This is the 'personal AI' vision maturing faster in China than the West.
items.try_this: If you have routine tasks you do daily (email triage, briefings, monitoring), consider whether an agent could take them over. The OpenClaw heartbeat system is a version of this.

items.title: Coinbase deploying Slack-native AI agents
items.body: Agents handling internal tasks via chat. Brian Armstrong highlighting early deployments. Enterprise agents going from 'experimental' to 'embedded in workflow tools' is the adoption signal to watch.

openclaw_agents.try_today: Tesla Q1 earnings are Tuesday (April 22). Key things to watch: Robotaxi unit economics, FSD subscription numbers, Megapack deployment growth, and any Terafab timeline updates. Stock at $400 — was $352 a month ago.

tesla.note: Holding $400 ahead of Q1 earnings Tuesday. Robotaxi expanding (3 Texas cities now fully driverless). Energy storage division projected 25% revenue increase, offsetting declining auto margins. Q1 revenue forecast: $21.2B. Megapack deployments at 8.8 GWh. Stock up from $352 a month ago.

tesla_spacex_xai.spacex: GPS III-8 ('Hedy Lamarr') launching tomorrow (April 21, 2:53 AM ET) from Cape Canaveral — stepping in after ULA Vulcan failure. First stage 7th flight. Also: $175.7M ESA contract for Falcon Heavy Rosalind Franklin Mars rover launch (2028), but NASA FY2027 budget cuts (~23%) threaten cancellation. Musk posted cryptic 'Ascension' on X.
tesla_spacex_xai.xai: French prosecutors summoned Musk and ex-X CEO Linda Yaccarino over X's alleged spread of CSAM, deepfakes, and Grok-generated Holocaust denial content. Probe ongoing since 2025, potentially timed ahead of SpaceX/IPO merger. No new model or product news.

items.title: Him (2025)
items.note: Dropped yesterday. Psychological thriller about a football prospect's trial with his idol. Jordan Peele produced. If you like elevated horror/thrillers, this has the right pedigree.

items.title: Funny AF with Kevin Hart
items.note: Four-episode premiere today. Kevin Hart stand-up series. If you like Hart, four eps is a quick binge. If he's not your thing, skip it.

items.title: Cheech & Chong's Last Movie
items.note: Exclusive today. If you're a Cheech & Chong fan, this is literally their last movie — worth the nostalgia. If you're not, probably not your entry point.

items.title: From — Season 4
items.note: Premiered yesterday. The horror/mystery series about a town that traps you. If you've been watching, you know. If you haven't, start from Season 1 — it rewards patience.

items.title: Tesla earnings Tuesday — key catalyst for any Tesla-related positioning
items.body: Q1 earnings April 22. Robotaxi expansion, energy storage growth, and Terafab chip updates are the angles. If InstaClaw has any Tesla/autonomy integration, this is the week to pay attention.

items.title: Agentic AI narrative is at peak tension — agents are everywhere AND obsolete
items.body: Enterprise adoption is up (43% in production), but Box CEO says they're obsolete in months. The winning positioning isn't 'we have agents' — it's 'we build agents that survive model upgrades.' If InstaClaw's architecture handles model swapping well, this is the messaging window.

macro.mood: Markets quiet (weekend). TSLA at $400 ahead of Tuesday earnings. LMND holding $70+ after strong week. BTC flat at $75K. All eyes on Tesla Q1 Tuesday and SpaceX GPS launch tomorrow. Good Friday was a market holiday — thin trading.
